rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TITO DANILO VARGAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/25/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please clarify the date of the accounting. Petition page 3 lines 26 and 27 allege the account begins July 1, 2020. The proposed order alleges the account begins July 1, 2020 on page 2 line 16. The summary of account alleges the account is 07/01/2019 to 02/28/2022 and appears to begin 07/01/2019. 2) The court will not grant this petition based upon information and belief Kemp & Associates, Inc is acting as an agent under power of attorney. (See petition page 5 paragraph 21.) The personal representative needs to make definitive statements regarding the agent under power of attorney for the listed individuals. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/11/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE NORMAN LLOYD PETERSEN REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name. 1) Court to determine how to proceed. Account ends 12/31/2021 with approximately $102,931.72 in assets. Petitioner is requesting to terminate the trust. 05/12/2022 Declaration says the trust authorized termination pursuant to Probate Code 15408 whenever the fair market value of the trust falls below $40k. This account ends 12/31/2021 with $102,931.72 assets on hand at end of the account period. 2) Trustee requests $11,589.00 be paid to court-appointed counsel Christopher Jew without joining in the petition or timesheets and Mr. Jew has filed a petition for fees in the conservatorship with a hearing date set for 07/11/2022. It appears this request should be withdrawn. 3) Attorney Kathryn A. Schofield paid herself twice for fees ordered on 01/02/2022 and 11/20/2022. No legal authority cited for interest owed instead attorney calculated interest at 1%. It appears the 10% judgment rate should apply. CCP 685.010(a).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN W. POLK
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: continued to july 25, 2022. new notice must be mailed and a new POS filed, as the notice of the amended petition was filed contemporaneously with the amended petition itself, which means either it was signed before notice was mailed, or notice was mailed prior to the pleading being filed (#5 says "I served" not I will serve). in addition, 13101 affidavits must be filed over the counter so the court can verify the death certificate is a certified copy (see LRSF 14.59.c.2). deadline for curing all issues is noon on 7/18/22. please note, examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF LEONISA A. TRIAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Despite Counsel's projection on 5/11/22 that the issues would be addressed by the end of that week, nothing has been filed to address those issues and no Notice of Conservatee's Death has been filed either. A search of the Court's register of actions did not reveal a new estate case opened for this conservatee. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 11th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN BARRY BALDWIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order For Preliminary Distribution, To Pay Creditor Claims, And For Release Of Funds From Escrow
rulings.Rulings: continued to july 11, 2022. the court is not in a position to approve distribution when the final I & A has not been filed, and the request is for significant cash distributions when there is no cash on any of the I & As filed so far. as the PRs appraise the cash on Attachment 1, without the need for a referee, and the second supplement states all bank statements have been obtained, there is no reason for any further delay in getting the final I & A on file. if filed by noon on 7/5/22, the examiner can set the petition for hearing (no longer able to pregrant preliminary distributions) and the court can set a date for status on filing the first interim account,

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JOSE QUINTANILLA
rulings.Calendar Matter: FILING OF FIRST ACCOUNT
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Since the conservatee died in March 2021, the conservator has not filed a petition for approval of a final report on waiver of account and termination of the conservatorship estate. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 11th Amended General Order which took effect on 04/25/2022 and LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE ONG FAMILY TRUST DATED MAY 28, 2002
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Petition For: 1) Order For Accounting; 2) Order For Damages, Attorney'S Fees And Costs For Failure To Timely Provide Notice Of Trust Under Pc
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/01/2022. 1) No proof of service of notice of the amended petition has been filed. 2) The verification must refer to the "amended" petition. 3) As to the relief under Probate Code sections 850 & 859, notice (which must be on the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-115) with a copy of the amended petition must be served on the trustees in the manner required for service of a summons in a civil action. See section 851(a)(1). 4) No copy of the trust was attached to the amended petition. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KANTABEN IL PATEL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/20/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Petitioner must provide the required courtesy copies of the 05/18/2022 and 05/20/2022 filings. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/5/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE NORA C. O'CONNOR 1991 REVOCABLE LVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Process, And For Dismissal, Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Respondent's contention that personal service of this petition was required is incorrect, as is her citation to Probate Code section 851. The instant petition is brought under Probate Code section 17200, not section 850. Probate Code section 15642 provides the grounds for removal of a trustee. Subdivision (a) of that section provides that a beneficiary may file a petition seeking removal of the trustee under Probate Code section 17200. Section 17203 dictates how notice and service of a petition under section 17200 shall be given. For a trustee, section 17203(a) requires that at "least 30 days before the time set for the hearing on the petition, the petitioner shall cause notice of the hearing to be delivered pursuant to Section 1215 []" Probate Code Section 1215 expressly permits "[d]elivery by mail," and specifies the method of mailing. Petitioner submitted a proof of service by mail which satisfies the requirements of section 1215. (See O'Sullivan Declaration, Exhibit A.) Accordingly, the motion to quash service of summons is DENIED. An alternative basis for denying the Motion to Quash is mootness. Trustee Mary Ellen O'Connor states in her supplemental declaration to this motion that she "will file a complete Response to the Petition which is now set for hearing on July 11, 2022" (Supplemental Declaration at P 10.) in the next paragraph she asks the Court to "defer any issues" concerning the pending petition until the July 11 hearing. (Supplemental Declaration, P 11.) The Court finds that, through the above statement and request, Trustee Mary Ellen O'Connor has indicated that she will file a brief in this action and has asked the Court to take an action - to wit, deferring granting any relief until the hearing scheduled for July 11. These actions are inconsistent with the position taken in the instant motion that she is not properly before the Court due to a lack of proper service. Trustee Mary Ellen O'Connor has effectively appeared in the case through her Supplemental Declaration. Further, on its own motion pursuant to Probate Code section 15642(b)(1) and (4), the Court hereby SUSPENDS Trustee Mary Ellen O'Connor effective immediately. Vincent O'Connor and Rose Abendroth are appointed as temporary co-trustees, effective immediately. Pursuant to Probate Code section 15642(f) the Court orders Trustee Mary Ellen O'Connor to surrender all trust property (including the trust bank account) to temporary co-trustees Vincent O'Connor and Rose Abendroth. The surrender of assets must be completed within 5 days of the entry of this order. Petitioners are ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling, as published above, by the time set for this hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day before the hearing. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHRISTOPHER CHARLES HEFNER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required on the sole issue of whether this petition should be taken off calendar for lack of progress and failure to address the following issues e-mailed to the petitioner on 04/22/2022: 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) No proof of publication is on file. 3) No DE-147 form is on file. 4) No DE-147S form is on file. This form must be filed conventionally and confidentially with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed or attached to any e-filed document). 5) The petition caption: The box "Authorization to Administer" must be checked, as item 2c of the petition requests full IAEA authority. 6) Items 3a & 3d of the petition: The decedent died more than 2 years ago, and the estimated value of the estate is $0. Please explain why this probate was opened at this time. 7) Item 3e(3): A bond waiver signed by the decedent's father is not on file. 8) Item 3g(1)(c) must be unchecked, as the decedent died intestate. 9) Item 8: A declaration of diligence required by CRC (California Rules of Court) 7.52(a) must be filed to describe efforts made to locate and contact the decedent's father. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 11th Amended General Order which took effect on 04/25/2022 and LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KEVIN NGUYEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) The petition states that the will is dated 4/16/18 (which is the date of the witnesses' signatures); however, the will states that Decedent signed the will on 4/15/18. The will also states that the trust is dated 4/15/18. Declaration by the attorney indicates this was a clerical error and that she and the other witness observed Decedent sign the will on 4/16/18. 2) The trust beneficiaries have been identified in the declaration filed on 6/7/22. Petitioner has not alleged how those listed are related to Decedent. In addition, neither Petitioner nor Decedent's children are listed as trust beneficiaries. Last, all interested parties, including Petitioner are listed as having the same address. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 11th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 11th amended general order.
